Entertainment

Paris 2024 Olympics: Bollywood Celebs Deepika Padukone, Sunil Shetty and Ajay Devgn Gives Shout Out to Indian Olympic Team, calls 'pride of our nation'

As India gears up for the 2024 Summer Olympics, which is being held in Paris this year, Bollywood celebrities, including Alia Bhatt, Deepika Padukone, Ajay Devgn, and Sunil Shetty took to social media to extend their support and best wishes. They cheered on the team, encouraging them as they embarked on their Olympic mission.

Actress Deepika Padukone posted a video on Instagram from the 2024 Olympics opening ceremony on July 26. The video shows Indian athletes PV Sindhu and Sharath Kamal carrying the Indian flag and leading the team during the Parade of Nations, which took place on a boat on the River Seine in Paris, France.

Deepika Padukone Cheers Indian Team By Sharing Video



During the opening ceremony of the Olympics, 78 Indian athletes and team members sailed on a boat, enthusiastically waving the Indian flag and cheering for the country. They proudly held the Tricolor high, showing their national spirit. Deepika Padukone's Instagram post had a special connection - her father. The iconic badminton player Prakash Padukone, was part of the Indian contingent on the boat. She captioned her post with #Olympics2024 and chose the energetic songLehra Dofrom the film83to match the excitement of the moment.

ALSO READ: Paris 2024 Olympics: Beyonce Introduces Team USA, Hypes Athletes Saying, 'We’ve Got Legends'

Suniel Shetty showed his support for India's athletes on Instagram. He posted a story with a poster of Indian athletes at the Paris 2024 Olympics and wrote, "Wishing our incredible athletes the very best for the Paris Olympics. The nation stands behind you as you #GoForGlory. Jai Hind."

Actor Suniel Shetty wishes India ahead of Olympic expedition



Ajay Devgn posted on X (formerly Twitter) to show his support for the Indian athletes competing in the Olympics 2024. He called them the "pride of our nation" and said they are the "best" at what they'll do, expressing his confidence in their performance. The actor wrote, "be assured that we will be cheering our hearts out to see perform. It's time to bring home the hardware. Cheers and good luck!"

Alia Bhatt also took to her Instagram handle as she cheered for Team India and wrote, "Onwards and Upwards Team India (sic)."



For the unversed, French President Emmanuel Macron officially opened the Paris Games, followed by French judo champion Teddy Riner and former track and field star Marie-Jose Perec.
